text: Chemical-induced forms of Fanconi syndrome have been described. As in congenital forms, these are characterised by a loss of water and solutes in the urine due to insufficient reabsorption at the proximal tubule. Chemicals that show such an effect on the apical to basolateral transport in proximal tubule cells in vitro are thus likely to have this effect in vivo. Other aspects of the etiology that are not modeled by in vitro proximal tubule models are bone-related illnesses and weight faltering.
